scenario: Lobsters work session - the shared vote/score recalculation (tight)

steps.step: Locate the recalculation and how its target is resolved

map_quality.rationale: Pins the call site exactly at vote.rb:181 with the guarding context and resolves the target at vote.rb:185-191 with the Comment.find/Story.find branch — exactly what the rubric asks for.

specificity.rationale: Both the call site (`t.update_score_and_recalculate!` at vote.rb:181) and the target-resolution lines (vote.rb:177 `t = v.target`; vote.rb:185-191) are pinned with concrete file:line.

justification.rationale: Explicitly notes 'TWO implementations ... dispatched polymorphically via v.target' and that the recalc 'runs on either a Comment or a Story depending on what was voted on', explaining why the call site cannot statically pick a definition.

uncertainty.rationale: Does not flag any hop it left unopened or partially verified; presents everything with uniform confidence even though step 1's scope is narrower than what was answered.

relationship.rationale: Clearly states the target is resolved to one of two record kinds via `comment_id ? Comment.find(comment_id) : Story.find(story_id)`, making the polymorphic dispatch path explicit.

steps.step: Document what the recalculation recomputes and persists

map_quality.rationale: Pins both divergent definitions exactly: Comment at comment.rb:339-363 with calculated_confidence at comment.rb:232, writing score/flags/confidence/confidence_order; Story at story.rb:693-718 with calculated_hotness at story.rb:550, writing score/flags/hotness. No conflation.

specificity.rationale: Per-kind methods and columns are named with exact file:line — `calculated_confidence` at comment.rb:232-251 plus `confidence_order` packing, `calculated_hotness` at story.rb:550-580, and the SQL UPDATE line ranges (comment.rb:355-362, story.rb:697-716).

justification.rationale: Explains the two value methods substantively (Wilson 80% lower bound vs tag hotness_mod + age decay) and notes SQL re-derives score/flags from votes rather than the in-memory delta, which is why the per-kind columns diverge. Could state the per-thread-confidence vs front-page-hotness purpose contrast more directly.

uncertainty.rationale: Does not explicitly flag any unopened hop or uncertain column attribution; given the answer is comprehensive this is partial credit, but no calibration statement is offered.

relationship.rationale: Cleanly attributes each branch to its own definition and cascade — Comment→calculated_confidence→{score,flags,confidence,confidence_order}; Story→calculated_hotness→{score,flags,hotness} — with the polymorphic `v.target` dispatch named explicitly. No cross-contamination.

steps.step: Map the change and the specs that cover it

map_quality.rationale: Names both files to edit (comment.rb:339-363, story.rb:693-718), the single call site (vote.rb:181) with target resolution (vote.rb:185), and concrete spec ranges across vote_spec.rb, story_spec.rb (including #calculated_hotness at 373-401 and update_score_and_recalculate at 404-429), and comment_spec.rb (confidence at 226-243). The agent has a tight edit-and-test map.

specificity.rationale: Every file is pinned to exact line ranges (e.g., 'spec/models/vote_spec.rb:15-82', 'spec/models/story_spec.rb:404-429', 'spec/models/comment_spec.rb:226-243'); no vague 'add tests'.

justification.rationale: Each spec entry is annotated with what it asserts (transitions, flag-deducts-from-score, calculated_hotness exact values, confidence ordering), and the closing 'regression_risk' note explains why the vote_spec transitions + value-method specs together form the guard.

uncertainty.rationale: No explicit flags of low-confidence hops or unopened paths; the answer presents the map as complete without naming any residual uncertainty (e.g., whether other indirect specs touch the recalc).

relationship.rationale: Each definition is kept tied to its own value method, columns, and specs: comment.rb edits ↔ calculated_confidence ↔ comment_spec.rb confidence tests; story.rb edits ↔ calculated_hotness ↔ story_spec.rb hotness tests; no conflation between the two branches.
